title: Tender Spicy French Chuck Roast Galette
description: A hearty and flavorful Normandy-inspired dish featuring tender chuck roast, perfect for a post-workout dinner. This satvik-friendly galette is spicy and gooey, made with pressure-cooked chuck roast and a flaky crust.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large pressure cooker over medium-high heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until browned, about 5 minutes.
Add the garlic, thyme, cayenne pepper, salt, and black pepper, and cook for 1 minute.
Add the chuck roast chunks and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add 2 cups of water to the pressure cooker and bring to a boil.
Close the lid and cook for 30 minutes, or until the meat is tender.
Let the pressure release naturally, then open the lid and shred the meat with two forks.
In a large bowl, combine the flour and cold water, and mix until a dough forms.
Roll out the dough into a large circle, about 1/4 inch thick.
Spoon the shredded meat onto one half of the dough, leaving a 1/2 inch border around the edges.
Fold the other half of the dough over the meat, and press the edges to seal.
